title: Could a name like U-Haul be an overlooked stock pick?
content:
Yacktman Asset Management president Molly Pieroni explains why one name in particular is an excellent overlooked stock pick.

Video Transcript

00:00 Speaker A

I know you got another stock you like from a valuation perspective, and that is U-Haul. What do you like about UHAL?

00:07 Speaker B

We're actually one of the largest outside shareholders of U-Haul. and uh it's a it's a really interesting story and a and an interesting company. It's family-owned and uh and controlled, which uh and and we like it for that reason because they are making capital investments in the company as business owners would.

00:30 Speaker B

They're they're taking the the the cash flow from the trucking business and they're investing in self-storage that's that they've kind of in a in a in a very quiet and probably too quiet way have grown into one of the largest self-storage place players out there.

00:46 Speaker B

So they do have these two primary businesses, the DIY truck uh rental business, which uh which you mentioned. It's it's basically like the Kleenex brand for truck rental. When you move, you get a U-Haul. You don't get a rental truck, you get a U-Haul.

01:05 Speaker B

Um and then they've built the self- storage business, and it's a really good strong cash flow business that the public storage REITs have um obviously enjoyed and and and played a a good role in uh in returns in that business. But because you've got a family-run business that's not particularly transparent yet about how they um how they report their line of business financials.

01:34 Speaker B

You've got a company that's doing all the right things when we look at it from an NAV perspective and how they are building the company brick by brick and in ways that are really good for long-term value.

01:46 Speaker B

They aren't talking about it and describing it in ways yet that the uh the the normal Wall Street investors and analysts would be tracking um and understand. It just doesn't fall well into into into either bucket.

title: A Look At U‑Haul Holding’s (UHAL) Valuation As Short Term Share Momentum Returns
content:
Get insights on thousands of stocks from the global community of over 7 million individual investors at Simply Wall St.

Without a clear single news headline driving attention, U-Haul Holding (UHAL) is drawing interest as investors weigh its recent share performance, value metrics, and its position in the do-it-yourself moving and storage market.

See our latest analysis for U-Haul Holding.

The recent 7 day share price return of 8.29% and 1 month share price return of 8.04% contrast with a 1 year total shareholder return decline of 16.10%, which suggests that short term momentum is picking up after a weaker longer term period.

If you are comparing U-Haul with other ideas in the transport and infrastructure ecosystem, it may be worth scanning 30 power grid technology and infrastructure stocks

With U-Haul Holding trading at $51.21 against an analyst price target of $75.45 and an intrinsic value signal implying a premium, investors are left to ask: Is this a genuine value opportunity, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Price-to-Earnings of 78.1x: Is it justified?

Based on the preferred valuation yardstick, U-Haul Holding currently trades on a P/E of 78.1x, which looks rich compared with both peers and the wider US transportation space.

The P/E multiple compares the company’s share price with its earnings per share and is often used for established, profit making businesses. For U-Haul, this lens is particularly relevant because earnings quality is described as high, even though recent profit trends have been weak.

U-Haul’s earnings have declined by 21.3% per year over the past 5 years and profit margins are currently 2.1%, lower than the 7.9% level reported last year. Against that backdrop, a 78.1x P/E suggests the market is paying a high price relative to current earnings, despite no supporting information here on strong profit growth forecasts.

Compared with its immediate peer group average P/E of 56.2x and the broader US Transportation industry average of 40.5x, U-Haul’s 78.1x multiple stands out as significantly higher. That gap indicates investors are assigning a premium valuation to the shares versus sector norms.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

Result: Price-to-Earnings of 78.1x (OVERVALUED)

However, weak multi year total returns and a 2.1% profit margin leave little room for disappointment if earnings or demand soften from here.

Find out about the key risks to this U-Haul Holding narrative.

Another View: Cash Flows Point To A Different Story

While the P/E ratio paints U-Haul Holding as expensive, the SWS DCF model gives another angle. At $51.21, the shares are trading above an estimated future cash flow value of $41.59, which also signals overvaluation and raises a different question: are investors leaning too hard on optimistic assumptions?

title: Grand Opening: U-Haul of Rossford Offers Climate-Controlled Storage Next to I-75
content:
Facility will create 10 jobs and provide a price lock on self-storage rentals

ROSSFORD, Ohio, March 31, 2026--(BUSINESS WIRE)--A new U-Haul® retail, moving and storage facility is now serving the Rossford community at 10212 Sportsman Drive while offering the Company’s 1-Year Price Lock on self-storage rentals.

Rossford Mayor Neil MacKinnon III participated in the ribbon-cutting ceremony on March 28.

The grand opening marked the completion of the first 431 climate-controlled self-storage rooms available to rent at U-Haul Moving & Storage of Rossford. Do-it-yourself movers have had access to trucks and trailers since December.

When the build-out is completed, there will be 1,067 storage units with heated and climate-controlled options designed to protect tenants’ belongings during Northwest Ohio’s four distinct seasons.

U-Haul of Rossford sits on nine acres next to Interstate 75 and one mile from I-90, offering easy access to residents and businesses.

"We’re pleased to provide the community with clean, dry and secure storage along with all of the reliable U-Haul moving products that people trust in one convenient location," said Chris Arndt, U-Haul Company of NW Ohio president. "U-Haul is excited to be in Rossford. We appreciate the city’s work, support and cooperation in making this happen."

Arndt was joined at the grand opening by Desiree Johnson, general manager of the Rossford store, and Josh Stevens, U-Haul traffic control manager.

This is the first U-Haul-owned and -operated center in Rossford. The Company has nine stores in neighboring Toledo.

Store Details and Offerings

In addition to indoor self-storage, U-Haul of Rossford offers:

A spacious retail showroom featuring an array of moving boxes, packing supplies, towing accessories and more. Three covered drive-in load/unload bays to safeguard storage customers and their belongings from the weather. A U-Box® container warehouse with capacity for 689 portable moving and storage containers. A hitch installation bay serviced by certified U-Haul hitch pros. Green initiatives like the Take A Box, Leave A Box® program and a Re-Use Center where donated goods in suitable condition can be shared among community members.

The Sportsman Drive location features two buildings: the 127,832-square-foot customer facility and a separate U-Box warehouse. U-Haul acquired the land in January 2023 and developed it to meet the increasing demand for storage and moving services in the Rossford area.

The store is expected to create 10 jobs when fully staffed. U-Haul will be looking to hire hitch pros, storage housekeepers and customer service representatives for this location. U-Haul always aims to hire locally to support the communities in which it does business. Veterans and military members receive preference in the hiring process. Visit uhauljobs.com to apply for careers in your area.

Story Continues

U-Haul 1-Year Price Lock

Storage tenants who want cost assurance without surprise rent increases, junk fees and deposits should inquire ask about the U-Haul 1-Year Price Lock. While other national operators face rising scrutiny for deceptive introductory rates followed by recurring price hikes and hidden fees, U-Haul is recommitting to its customers with fixed-rate storage and the certainty of no increases for at least a year. Read what U-Haul CEO Joe Shoen told Modern Storage Media in this exclusive interview: https://www.modernstoragemedia.com/msm-exclusives/joe-shoen-deceptive-pricing-practices.

About U-HAUL

Founded in 1945, U-Haul is the No. 1 choice of do-it-yourself movers with more than 24,000 rental locations across all 50 states and 10 Canadian provinces. The U-Haul app makes it easy for customers to use U-Haul Truck Share 24/7 to access trucks anytime through the self-dispatch and -return options on their smartphones with our patented Live Verify technology. Our customers' patronage has enabled the U-Haul fleet to grow to approximately 203,000 trucks, 137,400 trailers and 41,700 towing devices. U-Haul, which offers rate transparency to self-storage customers through its 1-Year Price Lock, is the third largest storage operator in North America with 1,126,800 rentable storage units and 98 million square feet of self-storage space at owned and managed facilities. U-Haul is the top retailer of propane in the U.S. and the largest installer of permanent trailer hitches in the automotive aftermarket industry. Get the U-Haul app from the App Store or Google Play.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260331367341/en/

title: U-Haul Holding Company (UHAL): Billionaire David Abrams Continues To Be Bullish
content:
U-Haul Holding Company (NYSE:UHAL) is one of the 12 Best Stocks to Buy According to Billionaire David Abrams.

U-Haul Holding Company (NYSE:UHAL) has been a constant feature in the 13F portfolio of Abrams Capital Management since late 2022. Back then, this position consisted of 3.6 million shares. In early 2024, this holding was trimmed more than 3%, and shares owned went down to 3.5 million. Further reductions were made in the third and fourth quarters of 2024, by 4% and 4.5% respectively, to bring ownership down to 3.25 million shares. The latest filings for the fourth quarter of 2025 show no changes to this position. The firm operates as a do-it-yourself moving and storage operator for household and commercial goods in the United States and Canada. It rents trucks, trailers, fixed and portable moving and storage units, specialty rental items, and self-storage spaces primarily to the household movers, and sells moving supplies, towing accessories, and propane.5 Best Tech Stocks Under $50 to Buy Now

A person with a cell phone who is looking for new stocks

U-Haul Holding Company (NYSE:UHAL) has emerged as a deep value play for elite hedge funds in recent years. Several factors explain this position. For example, the company is dominating the self-storage business. It owns approximately 98 million square feet of self-storage space. For U-Haul, the moving business is cyclical, but the storage segment is a recurring revenue powerhouse. U-Haul Chairman Joe Shoen indicated in the February 2026 earnings call that the company is aggressively right-sizing the fleet by selling off older, high-mileage trucks. Funds are betting that once depreciation costs normalize in the latter half of 2026, U-Haul’s earnings will experience a significant snap-back effect.

While we acknowledge the potential of UHAL as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If you're looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also stands to benefit significantly from Trump-era tariffs and the onshoring trend, see our free report on the best short-term AI stock.

title: Disaster Relief: U-Haul Offers Help to Kona Storm Victims across Hawaii
content:
HONOLULU, March 18, 2026--(BUSINESS WIRE)--U-Haul Company of Hawaii is offering 30 days of free self-storage and U-Box container usage to residents impacted by last week’s Kona Storm and preparing for the next round of severe weather.

Torrential rainfall and hurricane-force winds produced flooding, landslides and extensive property damage across the islands. Another Kona Low is expected to hit the Aloha State later this week.

Public access to self-storage units and portable storage containers is essential to communities preparing for natural disasters and recovering after they strike.

Don Chae, U-Haul Co. of Hawaii president, said his team is ready to help anyone affected by the storm who needs a dry and secure storage solution at no cost for one month.

U-Haul has five centers currently offering self-storage in Hawaii: three on Oahu, one on Maui, and one on the Big Island.

The disaster relief program applies to new self-storage and U-Box rentals and is based on availability at participating locations. The U-Box offer is for on-site storage at Company facilities; delivery is available for a modest fee. Call or stop by the nearest U-Haul store to take advantage of the 30 days free goodwill offer.

In addition to its 30 days free self-storage disaster relief program, U-Haul is proud to be at the forefront of aiding communities in times of need as an official American Red Cross Disaster Responder.

For customers needing storage beyond the free period, the U-Haul 1-Year Price Lock is now available at 2,100 Company-owned facilities across the U.S. and Canada. Fixed-rate storage ensures at least one year with no price increase on your rental unit, and U-Haul never charges junk fees or deposits. Learn more at uhaul.com/Storage/1-Year-Price-Lock.

About U-HAUL

Founded in 1945, U-Haul is the No. 1 choice of do-it-yourself movers with more than 24,000 rental locations across all 50 states and 10 Canadian provinces. The U-Haul app makes it easy for customers to use U-Haul Truck Share 24/7 to access trucks anytime through the self-dispatch and -return options on their smartphones with our patented Live Verify technology. Our customers' patronage has enabled the U-Haul fleet to grow to approximately 203,000 trucks, 137,400 trailers and 41,700 towing devices. U-Haul, which offers rate transparency to self-storage customers through its 1-Year Price Lock, is the third largest storage operator in North America with 1,126,800 rentable storage units and 98 million square feet of self-storage space at owned and managed facilities. U-Haul is the top retailer of propane in the U.S. and the largest installer of permanent trailer hitches in the automotive aftermarket industry. Get the U-Haul app from the App Store or Google Play.
